# AD.LIB Format Library — Full Reference

> 18 formats · 60 sub-formats · 3 funnel stages · 3 media types

## How to Read This Reference

Each format has:
- **Media type** — `video`, `image`, or `mixed`
- **Funnel stage** — `top` (awareness), `mid` (consideration), `btm` (conversion)
- **Sub-formats** — specific executions of the format
- **Structure** — the narrative formula
- **Hook pattern** — the attention-grabbing opener
- **Dica** — production tip

## The Formats

<!-- Format data auto-generated from packages/format-engine/src/formats.ts -->
<!-- See the actual TypeScript source for the authoritative data. -->

### Quick Reference

| Stage | Video | Image/Mixed |
|-------|-------|-------------|
| **TOP** (Awareness) | News Format, Interview, Skit, POV, Question Box, Reaction, Native UGC, Green Screen, Trend Hijack, Storytelling, Listicle | Static/Image, Split Screen |
| **MID** (Consideration) | Talking Head, Fake Podcast, POV, Reaction, Split Screen, Native UGC, Demo, Narration | Static/Image, Split Screen |
| **BTM** (Conversion) | Talking Head, Split Screen, Demo, Direct Offer, Narration | Static/Image, Urgency |

### Production Tips by Format

- **Talking Head**: "Hook must land in first 3 seconds. Use chyrons to reinforce key claims."
- **Fake Podcast**: "Good lighting and decent mic are non-negotiable. Podcast format sets a high expectation."
- **News Format**: "The chyron matters more than the visual. News viewer reads first."
- **Street Interview**: "The question MUST be answerable in 1 sentence. 'Describe how you...' not 'Tell me about...'"
- **POV**: "Use stabilization (Horizon Lock). The visual must clearly contrast before vs after."
- **Split Screen**: "The wrong side must be genuinely painful to watch. Soft contrasts produce soft results."
- **Demo**: "The 'Aha moment' must happen in under 6 seconds. Zoom into the screen at the key moment."
- **Static/Image**: "If it doesn't work as a static ad, it won't work as a video. Test static first."
